signals.title: AUD/USD — Geopolitical Headline + Positioning + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7252 testing the 0.7260 resistance zone as Trump signals an end to Iran naval hostilities, easing immediate energy supply fears. AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is 89% short—a massive institutional vs. retail divergence. This extreme retail fade suggests a potential capitulation squeeze if price clears 0.7260. Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35% provides a -60bp carry advantage to AUD, further supporting the bid. Watch for US CPI data to confirm the USD depreciation path.

signals.title: GBP/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP/JPY at 214.39 reflects a dominant G8 bilateral theme as GBP outperforms (+0.512%) and JPY lags (-0.553%) across the G8 complex. BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% provides a +300bp carry premium, sustaining the pair's bid despite normal volatility (VIX 18.1). JPY COT LF net short -61,340 confirms a crowded carry trade; however, the 1.065% G8 spread suggests the move is stretched. Watch for BoJ verbal intervention if JPY weakness accelerates toward the 160.00 psychological level in USD/JPY.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at 18.5% extreme) shows speculators are heavily fading the New Zealand Dollar despite a hawkish RBNZ market bias. With the RBNZ next meeting on 27 May and a market bias toward a hike, a significant short-squeeze risk exists if the central bank reaffirms its tightening stance. NZD/USD at 0.5963 remains vulnerable to a sharp move higher as crowded spec shorts unwind. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% currently provides a +150bp USD carry advantage, but the positioning extreme outweighs the carry deficit.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Technical + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7779 continues to hold below its 100-hour moving average following a 1.71% decline over the last month. Retail sentiment is 87% long, attempting to catch the falling knife against a clear institutional trend (COT LF net short -7,392). Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0% offers a massive +375bp carry advantage for USD, yet the pair remains under pressure, indicating that safe-haven flows or technical momentum are overriding the interest rate differential. Watch for a break above the 100-hour MA to invalidate the bearish bias.
